title: cassia italica
intro: Cassia italica, also known as Senna alexandrina or Alexandrian senna, is a plant species native to Africa and the Middle East, belonging to the legume family. This plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, particularly its laxative effects, which are attributed to the presence of anthraquinone glycosides. The health benefits of Cassia italica include relief from constipation, bloating, and other gastrointestinal issues, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Cassia italica can be prepared in various forms, including tea, capsules, and powder, which are available over-the-counter in many health food stores and online.
